identity.title: Power-On Reset Detector
identity.supported_scope: pure voltage-domain event-driven transient behavioral Verilog-A

parameters.name: tr
parameters.override_behavior: sets output and metric transition smoothing

parameters.name: vth
parameters.override_behavior: sets clk and rst logic threshold

properties.observable_contract: Out is active-high reset and remains asserted while rst is high or vin is below vtrip.
After rst releases and vin is power-good, out stays asserted for four rising clk updates before deasserting.
Metric uses an intermediate status level during the release delay, is high after delayed reset release completes, and is cleared when reset is reasserted or supply is not power-good.
A new reset assertion or a brownout below vtrip immediately reasserts out and clears the accumulated release delay, independent of the next clk edge.
Out and metric use bounded voltage-coded low and high levels with finite transition smoothing.

modeling_constraints: Do not use current contributions, transistor-level devices, AC/noise analysis, or KCL/KVL regulation loops.
Do not encode a particular validation stimulus, stop time, or sample window.
